articles.headline: Historic Tripura Peace Accord Signed, Ends Decades-Old Insurgency
articles.explainer:
• A landmark peace accord was signed between the National Liberation Front of Tripura (NLFT) and the All Tripura Tiger Force (ATTF) on one side, and the State Government of Tripura and the Government of India on the other.
• The agreement marks the end of a 35-year-old insurgency in Tripura, bringing hopes for lasting peace and development in the region.
• The accord includes provisions for the rehabilitation and resettlement of former militants, surrender of arms, and integration into the mainstream.
• It emphasizes socio-economic development, protection of indigenous rights, and addressing the root causes of the insurgency.
• This follows a series of peace initiatives by the government in the North-Eastern states to resolve long-standing ethnic and political conflicts.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: The 2024 Tripura Peace Accord was signed on September 4, 2024.
Signatories include NLFT, ATTF, Government of Tripura, and Government of India.
The accord aims to end a 35-year insurgency in Tripura.
It focuses on rehabilitation, development, and protection of indigenous rights.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of peace accords in resolving insurgencies in North-East India, citing the Tripura accord as an example.
Analyze the multi-faceted approach required for sustainable peace, including rehabilitation, development, and addressing socio-economic grievances.
Evaluate the role of both central and state governments in fostering dialogue and achieving resolution in conflict-affected regions.
Examine the challenges that remain post-accord, such as ensuring full integration of former militants and sustained development.
articles.imageDescription: Signing ceremony of the Tripura Peace Accord with government officials and representatives of militant groups.
articles.trivia: Tripura shares a long international border with Bangladesh, which has historically complicated its internal security situation due to cross-border movement of insurgents.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues related to Security and Governance

articles.headline: UPSC Dismisses Trainee IAS Officer for Fraudulent Methods in Exam
articles.explainer:
• The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) dismissed and canceled the candidature of trainee IAS officer Puja Khedkar.
• The dismissal stems from her use of fraudulent methods to clear the prestigious UPSC Civil Services Examination.
• This action underscores UPSC's strict stance against malpractices and its commitment to maintaining the integrity of the examination process.
• The incident highlights the importance of ethical conduct and honesty for individuals aspiring to join the civil services.
• Such cases often lead to systemic reviews within the examination and recruitment bodies to prevent future occurrences.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice and International relations
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies

Ethics and Human Interface: Essence, Determinants and Consequences of Ethics in-human actions; Dimensions of ethics; Ethics – in private and public relationships. Human Values – lessons from the lives and teachings of great leaders, reformers and administrators; role of family, society and educational institutions in inculcating values.

Probity in Governance: Concept of Public Service; Philosophical basis of governance and probity; Information sharing and transparency in government, Right to Information, Codes of Ethics, Codes of Conduct, Citizen’s Charters, Work culture, Quality of service delivery, Utilization of public funds, challenges of corruption.
prelimsPoints: UPSC dismissed trainee IAS officer Puja Khedkar.
Reason for dismissal: fraudulent methods in clearing the UPSC exam.
UPSC is a constitutional body responsible for civil services recruitment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of integrity and ethical conduct in the civil services and the role of bodies like UPSC in upholding these values.
Analyze the implications of fraudulent practices in competitive examinations on public trust and the quality of governance.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the examination system and prevent malpractices, ensuring fairness and transparency.
Examine the ethical dilemmas faced by aspirants and the importance of a strong moral compass for future administrators (GS4 relevance).
articles.imageDescription: UPSC building in New Delhi, symbolizing integrity and governance.
articles.trivia: The UPSC conducts one of the toughest examinations globally, with a success rate typically less than 0.2% annually.

articles.headline: Fresh Clashes in Manipur's Jiribam District Claim Five Lives
articles.explainer:
• At least five people were killed in renewed clashes between the Kuki and Meitei communities in Jiribam District, Manipur.
• The violence is part of the ongoing 2023–2025 Manipur violence, which has seen intermittent but severe ethnic strife.
• Jiribam, bordering Assam, has recently emerged as a new flashpoint in the conflict, previously being relatively peaceful.
• Security forces have been deployed, but the underlying tensions and cycle of violence continue to pose significant challenges to peace and order.
• The incident highlights the urgent need for a lasting political solution and effective security measures to de-escalate the conflict.
gsSubTopics: Internal Security
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: New clashes occurred in Jiribam District, Manipur.
Conflict involves Kuki and Meitei communities.
Jiribam is a district bordering Assam in Manipur.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complex socio-political and historical factors contributing to the ongoing ethnic violence in Manipur.
Discuss the challenges faced by security forces and the administration in controlling the violence and restoring peace.
Evaluate the role of state and central governments in addressing the root causes of the conflict and ensuring rehabilitation of affected communities.
Suggest long-term strategies for peacebuilding and reconciliation in conflict-ridden regions like Manipur.
articles.imageDescription: Security personnel patrolling a village affected by ethnic clashes in Manipur.
articles.trivia: Manipur is known as the 'Land of Jewels' and is one of the seven sister states in Northeast India, rich in biodiversity and cultural heritage.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Moderation
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision aligns with the RBI's primary objective of bringing inflation within the target range while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC emphasized the need for continued vigilance on inflation, especially given global uncertainties and domestic food price pressures.
• Projections for GDP growth and inflation were reiterated, with a cautious optimism about the trajectory of the Indian economy.
• This 'wait and watch' approach signals a data-dependent stance, indicating future policy actions will be guided by evolving economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged.
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims for 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in managing inflation, particularly due to supply-side factors like food price shocks.
Analyze the implications of a 'status quo' monetary policy on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Examine the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy for achieving macroeconomic stability.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Enhanced Strategic Partnership at ASEAN Summit
articles.explainer:
• India participated in the annual ASEAN Summit, reaffirming its commitment to strengthening the comprehensive strategic partnership with the bloc.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in trade, investment, digital transformation, connectivity, and maritime security.
• India's 'Act East Policy' was highlighted as a cornerstone of its engagement with Southeast Asian nations, promoting regional stability and prosperity.
• Leaders emphasized the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, with ASEAN centrality as a key principle.
• New initiatives were explored to boost people-to-people exchanges, cultural ties, and cooperation in sustainable development and climate action.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India is a dialogue partner of ASEAN and has a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
ASEAN consists of 10 Southeast Asian nations.
India's 'Act East Policy' aims to strengthen ties with ASEAN countries.
The concept of Indo-Pacific and ASEAN centrality are key themes in India-ASEAN relations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in the context of its strategic engagement with ASEAN.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in areas such as trade, maritime security, and digital economy.
Examine how India and ASEAN can collectively contribute to a stable, prosperous, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the impact of global geopolitical shifts on India's relations with Southeast Asian countries.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and ASEAN nations at a summit meeting.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by its five founding members.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Key Parachute Deployment Test for Gaganyaan Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test of the parachute deployment system for its Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission.
• This test is a critical milestone, validating the system designed to safely bring back the crew module and astronauts to Earth.
• The multi-parachute system ensures controlled descent and soft landing, a vital aspect of crew safety for India's maiden human space mission.
• Gaganyaan aims to send a three-member crew to a Low Earth Orbit (LEO) for three days and bring them back safely to Earth.
• This achievement reinforces India's growing capabilities in advanced space technology and its commitment to indigenous human spaceflight.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested the parachute deployment system for Gaganyaan.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
The mission aims to send a three-member crew to Low Earth Orbit.
The parachute system is crucial for the safe return and soft landing of the crew module.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Gaganyaan mission for India's space program and its broader implications for national development and global standing.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in human spaceflight, particularly focusing on re-entry and crew recovery systems.
Evaluate the role of indigenous development in achieving self-reliance in critical space technologies and its impact on India's scientific capabilities.
Examine the potential spin-off benefits of human spaceflight missions for various sectors, including healthcare, materials science, and education.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of the Gaganyaan crew module descending with parachutes deployed.
articles.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission's 'Vyommitra' is a female humanoid robot developed by ISRO, designed to fly aboard the uncrewed test flights to simulate human functions and monitor parameters.

articles.headline: Government Approves New Incentives to Boost Green Hydrogen Production
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new set of incentives and policy frameworks under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The move aims to accelerate domestic production of green hydrogen and its derivatives, positioning India as a global hub.
• Incentives include financial support for manufacturing electrolyzers and producing green hydrogen, alongside demand aggregation mechanisms.
• This initiative is crucial for India's energy security, reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports, and achieving net-zero emission targets by 2070.
• The mission is expected to create significant employment opportunities and foster technological innovation in the renewable energy sector.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Government approved new incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Aim is to boost green hydrogen production and electrolyzer manufacturing.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India targets net-zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and its role in achieving climate change mitigation targets.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production in India, including infrastructure, cost, and technological advancements.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government incentives and policy frameworks in attracting investment and fostering innovation in the green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen adoption on various industrial sectors and its contribution to job creation.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but 'green' hydrogen specifically refers to hydrogen produced with zero carbon emissions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Seeks Centre's Response on Electoral Bond Transparency Pleas
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India sought the Central government's response on several petitions challenging the legality and transparency of the Electoral Bond Scheme.
• The pleas argue that the scheme promotes opacity in political funding, violating citizens' right to information and free and fair elections.
• The Election Commission of India has also previously raised concerns about the lack of transparency under the scheme.
• This renewed judicial scrutiny comes amidst ongoing debates about electoral reforms and the need for greater accountability in political financing.
• The court's decision will have significant implications for the future of political funding in India and the democratic process.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court sought Centre's response on Electoral Bond Scheme.
Petitions challenge the scheme on grounds of transparency and right to information.
Election Commission of India has also expressed concerns about the scheme.
Electoral bonds are financial instruments for making donations to political parties.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the arguments for and against the Electoral Bond Scheme in the context of transparency, accountability, and democratic principles.
Discuss the role of the judiciary and the Election Commission in safeguarding the integrity of the electoral process and ensuring free and fair elections.
Examine various proposals for electoral reforms in India, particularly concerning political funding, and their potential impact.
Evaluate the balance between protecting donor privacy and ensuring public accountability in political donations.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The Electoral Bond Scheme was introduced in 2017 through the Finance Bill, 2017, and came into effect in 2018.

articles.headline: India Crosses Milestone in Digital Public Infrastructure Adoption
articles.explainer:
• India announced a significant milestone in the adoption and scaling of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) platforms, including UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker.
• The increased penetration reflects the success of government initiatives to promote financial inclusion, enhance public service delivery, and foster digital literacy.
• India's DPI model is gaining global recognition, with several countries expressing interest in replicating its successful frameworks.
• The expansion of platforms like ONDC (Open Network for Digital Commerce) is further democratizing digital markets and empowering small businesses.
• This widespread adoption is transforming various sectors, from banking and healthcare to education and governance, making services more accessible and efficient.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Policies & Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their Design and Implementation
prelimsPoints: India achieved a milestone in Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) adoption.
Key DPIs include UPI, Aadhaar, DigiLocker, and ONDC.
DPI aims to promote financial inclusion, public service delivery, and digital literacy.
India's DPI model is being recognized globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss how India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model has revolutionized financial inclusion and public service delivery, citing specific examples.
Analyze the opportunities and challenges in expanding India's DPI footprint globally, particularly in developing countries.
Evaluate the role of DPI in fostering innovation, economic growth, and social empowerment across various sectors.
Examine the ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with large-scale DPI implementation and suggest measures for robust governance.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the interconnectedness of various Digital Public Infrastructure platforms like UPI and Aadhaar.
articles.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) has set global benchmarks for real-time digital payments, processing billions of transactions monthly.

articles.headline: Progress Made on India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor Projects
articles.explainer:
• A review meeting confirmed significant progress on various projects under the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).
• IMEC, announced at the G20 Summit, aims to establish a vast network of rail, road, and sea linkages connecting India with Europe via the Middle East.
• The corridor is envisioned to enhance trade, improve logistics efficiency, and foster economic integration among participating nations.
• Discussions focused on accelerating infrastructure development, streamlining customs procedures, and mobilizing investments.
• The project holds strategic geopolitical significance, offering an alternative trade route and strengthening India's connectivity with key global regions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: IMEC is the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
It was announced at the G20 Summit.
Aims to connect India, Middle East, and Europe via rail, road, and sea.
Key objective is to enhance trade and logistics efficiency.
mainsPoints: Analyze the geopolitical and geo-economic significance of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) for India and the wider region.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges in the implementation of the IMEC, including infrastructure development, funding, and regional cooperation.
Compare and contrast IMEC with other major connectivity initiatives like China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI), highlighting their strategic implications.
Evaluate how IMEC can contribute to India's economic growth, trade diversification, and strategic autonomy.
articles.imageDescription: Map illustrating the proposed route of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
articles.trivia: The IMEC project was initially conceptualized as part of the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment (PGII), a G7 initiative to counter China's BRI.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Levels of Air Pollution in Indian Cities
articles.explainer:
• A recent report revealed alarming levels of air pollution, particularly PM2.5 concentrations, in several major Indian cities.
• The report highlighted the severe health impacts on urban populations, including respiratory diseases, cardiovascular issues, and reduced life expectancy.
• Key contributors to urban air pollution include vehicular emissions, industrial activities, construction dust, and biomass burning.
• The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) and other policy interventions are in place, but their implementation and effectiveness require significant strengthening.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for comprehensive, multi-sectoral strategies to improve air quality and protect public health.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Report highlights high PM2.5 levels in Indian cities.
PM2.5 refers to fine particulate matter 2.5 micrometers or less in diameter.
National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) is a government initiative to reduce air pollution.
Sources of air pollution include vehicles, industry, construction, and biomass burning.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes and consequences of escalating urban air pollution in India, particularly focusing on PM2.5 levels.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of existing government policies and programs, such as NCAP, in addressing air quality challenges.
Suggest comprehensive and integrated strategies, including technological, behavioral, and policy interventions, to mitigate air pollution in Indian cities.
Discuss the socio-economic implications of air pollution, including its impact on public health, productivity, and environmental justice.
articles.imageDescription: Smoggy skyline of an Indian city, depicting severe air pollution.
articles.trivia: Exposure to PM2.5 for even a few hours can cause respiratory and cardiac problems, while long-term exposure can lead to chronic diseases and premature death.

articles.headline: DAC Clears Major Defence Acquisitions to Boost Indigenous Production
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, approved several significant defence acquisition proposals.
• These approvals are aimed at bolstering the Indian armed forces' capabilities and promoting indigenous defence manufacturing under the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
• The procurements include advanced weapon systems, surveillance equipment, and platforms, with a strong emphasis on 'Make in India' components.
• The move is expected to reduce India's reliance on foreign defence imports and foster a robust domestic defence industrial base.
• Such decisions are crucial for India's strategic autonomy and enhancing its defence preparedness in the evolving geopolitical landscape.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) approves defence procurements.
DAC is chaired by the Defence Minister.
Focus on 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and 'Make in India' in defence.
Aims to reduce import dependence and boost indigenous production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and impact of India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in the defence sector on national security and economic development.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in enhancing indigenous defence manufacturing, including technology transfer, R&D, and private sector participation.
Evaluate how recent defence acquisition policies contribute to India's strategic autonomy and its standing as a regional power.
Examine the role of the Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) in streamlining the procurement process and ensuring transparency and efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Defence Minister reviewing military equipment at a defence exhibition.
articles.trivia: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Defence Ministry for deciding on new policies and capital acquisitions for the three services (Army, Navy, Air Force) and the Indian Coast Guard.

articles.headline: G20 Sherpas Discuss Agenda for Upcoming Leaders' Summit
articles.explainer:
• G20 Sherpas convened a crucial meeting to finalize the agenda and outcome documents for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions covered a wide range of global issues, including climate finance, sustainable development goals (SDGs), global debt, and digital public infrastructure.
• India, as a significant voice in the Global South, emphasized inclusive growth, equitable access to technology, and reforms in multilateral institutions.
• The Sherpas worked towards building consensus on key declarations and action plans that will guide global cooperation in the coming year.
• This preparatory meeting is vital for shaping the deliverables and overall success of the high-level G20 summit.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: G20 Sherpas meet to prepare for the Leaders' Summit.
Sherpas are personal representatives of the leaders of G20 countries.
Key agenda items include climate finance, SDGs, and global debt.
G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of the G20 as a premier forum for international economic cooperation and its significance for India's foreign policy.
Analyze the challenges in achieving consensus among G20 members on critical global issues such as climate change, trade, and debt sustainability.
Evaluate India's contributions and priorities within the G20 framework, particularly its advocacy for the Global South and reforms in global governance.
Examine the 'Sherpa track' process in global summits and its importance in diplomatic negotiations and outcome finalization.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo displayed at a conference venue.
articles.trivia: The term 'Sherpa' in diplomacy refers to the personal representatives of the leaders who prepare the agenda and negotiate the outcome of international summits.

articles.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Stricter Measures for Western Ghats Conservation
articles.explainer:
• An expert committee submitted recommendations for stricter environmental protection measures in the ecologically sensitive Western Ghats region.
• The panel emphasized the need to balance conservation efforts with the developmental aspirations of local communities.
• Recommendations include stricter regulations on mining, quarrying, and construction activities in designated ecologically sensitive areas (ESAs).
• The report revisits aspects of previous committees (Gadgil and Kasturirangan) on the Western Ghats, aiming for a more implementable framework.
• This move is crucial for preserving the rich biodiversity and ecological services provided by this UNESCO World Heritage site, which is a global biodiversity hotspot.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Environment
Disaster and disaster management
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Expert panel recommended stricter measures for Western Ghats conservation.
Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
Previous committees include the Gadgil Committee and Kasturirangan Committee.
Focus on regulating mining, quarrying, and construction in ESAs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the challenges in its conservation amidst developmental pressures.
Compare and contrast the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees on the Western Ghats, highlighting their strengths and weaknesses.
Discuss the socio-economic implications of conservation policies on local communities residing in ecologically sensitive areas.
Suggest a balanced approach to ensure sustainable development while preserving the biodiversity and ecological integrity of the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green mountains and forests of the Western Ghats.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is older than the Himalayas and is home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: Eight Killed in Lucknow Building Collapse, Investigation Underway
articles.explainer:
• At least eight people were killed and several others injured in a building collapse in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.
• Emergency services, including NDRF and SDRF teams, were swiftly deployed for rescue operations, pulling out survivors from the debris.
• Preliminary investigations suggest possible structural weaknesses, unauthorized construction, or use of substandard materials as potential causes.
• The incident highlights persistent issues related to urban planning, building code enforcement, and construction safety standards in rapidly developing cities.
• A high-level inquiry has been ordered to ascertain the exact cause and fix accountability for the tragic loss of lives.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Disaster and disaster management
mainsPoints: Discuss the underlying reasons for frequent building collapses in Indian cities, focusing on issues related to urban governance, regulatory oversight, and construction practices.
Analyze the role of disaster management agencies and local authorities in preventing such incidents and ensuring effective rescue and relief operations.
Suggest comprehensive reforms in urban planning, building codes, and enforcement mechanisms to enhance public safety in urban infrastructure.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such disasters on affected families and the broader community.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers searching through the debris of a collapsed building in Lucknow.
articles.trivia: The National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) is a specialized force constituted for the purpose of specialist response to natural and man-made disasters.

articles.headline: India's Startup Ecosystem Ranks Among Top Globally, NITI Aayog Report
articles.explainer:
• A NITI Aayog report highlighted India's robust growth in its startup ecosystem, ranking it among the top three globally in terms of the number of unicorns and total funding.
• The report attributed this growth to supportive government policies, increasing venture capital funding, and a large pool of skilled talent.
• Key sectors driving innovation include FinTech, EdTech, HealthTech, and DeepTech, contributing significantly to job creation and economic diversification.
• The 'Startup India' initiative and various incubation programs have played a crucial role in fostering an entrepreneurial culture.
• Challenges remain in scaling up, accessing late-stage funding, and navigating regulatory complexities, but the overall outlook remains positive.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Government Policies & Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their Design and Implementation
prelimsPoints: India's startup ecosystem ranks among the top three globally.
Growth attributed to government policies, VC funding, and skilled talent.
Key sectors include FinTech, EdTech, HealthTech, and DeepTech.
'Startup India' is a flagship government initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the rapid growth of India's startup ecosystem and its significance for job creation and economic diversification.
Discuss the role of government initiatives like 'Startup India' and NITI Aayog in promoting entrepreneurship and innovation.
Identify the major challenges faced by Indian startups, particularly in terms of funding, scaling, and regulatory environment.
Suggest policy measures to further strengthen the startup ecosystem, ensuring sustainable growth and global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant office space with young professionals working on laptops, symbolizing a startup environment.
articles.trivia: A 'unicorn' in the startup world refers to a privately held startup company valued at over $1 billion.

articles.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Continues Robust Growth, Targets Met
articles.explainer:
• India reported continued robust growth in its solar energy capacity, successfully meeting key targets for renewable energy deployment.
• This expansion is driven by supportive government policies, declining costs of solar technology, and increasing private sector investment.
• The enhanced solar capacity is crucial for achieving India's ambitious climate goals and transitioning to a cleaner energy mix.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, land acquisition, and ensuring round-the-clock power supply from intermittent sources.
• The focus is now shifting towards hybrid projects, energy storage solutions, and decentralized renewable energy for rural electrification.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India continues robust growth in solar energy capacity.
Driven by government policies and declining technology costs.
Crucial for achieving climate goals and cleaner energy mix.
Challenges include grid integration and energy storage.
mainsPoints: Assess India's progress in expanding its solar energy capacity and its contribution to global climate change mitigation efforts.
Discuss the policy and technological innovations that have driven the growth of solar power in India.
Analyze the challenges associated with large-scale integration of intermittent renewable energy sources into the national grid and proposed solutions.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of solar energy, including rural electrification, job creation, and energy access.
articles.imageDescription: A large solar power farm under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India's largest solar power plant is Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan, with a total capacity of over 2.2 GW.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Semiconductor Manufacturing in India
articles.explainer:
• The government announced a new comprehensive policy aimed at attracting global players and boosting domestic semiconductor manufacturing.
• The policy includes enhanced fiscal incentives, infrastructure support, and a streamlined regulatory framework for setting up fabrication units and design centers.
• This initiative is critical for India's strategic autonomy in electronics manufacturing and reducing dependence on global supply chains.
• It is expected to create a robust ecosystem for chip design, manufacturing, and packaging, fostering high-tech employment.
• The long-term vision is to position India as a key hub in the global semiconductor supply chain, attracting significant foreign direct investment.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Government unveiled a new policy for semiconductor manufacturing.
Aims to attract global players and boost domestic production.
Policy includes fiscal incentives and infrastructure support.
Crucial for India's strategic autonomy in electronics manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and national security.
Discuss the challenges India faces in establishing a competitive semiconductor industry, including high capital costs, technology access, and skilled workforce availability.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies and incentives in attracting investments and fostering local capabilities in chip design and fabrication.
Examine the potential impact of a thriving semiconductor industry on India's 'Make in India' initiative and its position in global supply chains.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of a silicon wafer with intricate chip designs.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is highly concentrated, with Taiwan and South Korea dominating advanced chip manufacturing.

highlights: Historic Tripura Peace Accord signed, ending a 35-year-old insurgency in the state, focusing on rehabilitation and development.
UPSC dismisses trainee IAS officer Puja Khedkar for fraudulent methods, reaffirming commitment to examination integrity.
Five killed in fresh Kuki-Meitei clashes in Manipur's Jiribam District, highlighting ongoing ethnic violence challenges.
RBI maintains status quo on repo rate, prioritizing inflation moderation while supporting economic growth.
ISRO successfully conducts a key parachute deployment test for the Gaganyaan mission, a critical step for human spaceflight.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 2024 Tripura Peace Accord, consider the following statements:
1. It was signed between the National Liberation Front of Tripura (NLFT) and the Government of India.
2. The accord aims to end a 35-year-old insurgency in Tripura.
3. It focuses solely on the surrender of arms by militant groups.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The accord was signed between NLFT and ATTF on one side, and the State Government of Tripura and the Government of India on the other. Statement 2 is correct: The accord specifically aims to end a 35-year-old insurgency in Tripura. Statement 3 is incorrect: The accord includes provisions for rehabilitation, resettlement, socio-economic development, and protection of indigenous rights, not solely the surrender of arms.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for the dismissal of a trainee IAS officer for fraudulent methods in the UPSC exam?
options: (a) Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) is the constitutional body responsible for conducting examinations for appointments to the All India Services and Central Services. It also has the authority to dismiss and cancel candidatures for malpractices, as per the rules and regulations governing the examination.

prelimsMocks.question:
The term 'Digital Public Infrastructure' (DPI) in India often refers to a set of platforms. Which of the following are considered key components of India's DPI?
1. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
2. Aadhaar
3. DigiLocker
4. Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC)
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All four mentioned platforms – UPI (for payments), Aadhaar (for identity), DigiLocker (for document management), and ONDC (for e-commerce) – are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure, facilitating various digital services and financial inclusion.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC):
1. It was announced at the G7 Summit.
2. It aims to connect India with Europe via the Middle East through a network of rail, road, and sea linkages.
3. Its primary objective is to counter China's Belt and Road Initiative.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: IMEC was announced at the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2023. Statement 2 is correct: IMEC is indeed envisioned as a multimodal corridor connecting India, the Middle East, and Europe. Statement 3 is incorrect: While it may serve as an alternative to BRI, stating its 'primary objective' is to counter BRI is not the official stance; its stated goals are to enhance trade, connectivity, and economic integration.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following committees are associated with the conservation of the Western Ghats?
1. Gadgil Committee
2. Kasturirangan Committee
3. Kothari Committee
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Gadgil Committee (Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel - WGEEP) and the Kasturirangan Committee (High-Level Working Group - HLWG) are both prominently associated with recommendations for the conservation and protection of the Western Ghats. The Kothari Committee is known for its recommendations on education reform.

mainsMocks.question: The signing of the Tripura Peace Accord signifies a crucial step towards resolving long-standing insurgencies in India's North-Eastern states. Analyze the multi-faceted approach required for sustainable peace in such conflict-affected regions, drawing lessons from recent accords. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Internal Security; Linkages between development and spread of extremism. GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Tripura Peace Accord and its significance. Discuss the 'multi-faceted approach' by breaking it down into components: dialogue & negotiation, rehabilitation & resettlement, socio-economic development, protection of indigenous rights, and addressing root causes. Provide examples of how these elements contribute to sustainable peace. Conclude with challenges and the need for continued vigilance.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambition to become a global hub for semiconductor manufacturing is critical for its strategic autonomy and economic growth. Discuss the key policy measures undertaken by the government to achieve this goal and the challenges that lie ahead. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment. Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Indigenization of technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by highlighting the strategic importance of semiconductors. Detail the government's policy measures (e.g., fiscal incentives, infrastructure, streamlined regulations, 'Make in India' integration). Discuss challenges such as high capital investment, technology access, skilled workforce, and global competition. Conclude with the potential benefits and the long-term vision.

mainsMocks.question: The persistent issue of urban air pollution in Indian cities poses significant public health and environmental challenges. Critically evaluate the effectiveness of existing government policies and programs, such as the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP), in addressing these challenges and suggest further measures. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment. GS1: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the problem of urban air pollution and its impacts. Briefly explain NCAP and other policies. Critically evaluate their effectiveness, noting successes and shortcomings (e.g., implementation gaps, data collection, source apportionment). Suggest further measures: multi-sectoral approach, stricter enforcement, public awareness, technology adoption, regional cooperation. Conclude with the urgency of comprehensive action.

quickBytes.text: The National Liberation Front of Tripura (NLFT) was formed in 1989.
The UPSC is a constitutional body established under Article 315 of the Indian Constitution.
The term 'repo rate' refers to the rate at which the central bank lends money to commercial banks.
India's 'Act East Policy' was launched in 2014, succeeding the 'Look East Policy' of 1991.
ISRO's Gaganyaan mission aims to send astronauts to a Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources for electrolysis of water.
Electoral bonds were introduced in India as a means of political funding in 2018.
The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
A 'unicorn' startup is a privately held company with a valuation exceeding $1 billion.

GS1.headline: Eight Killed in Lucknow Building Collapse, Investigation Underway
GS1.explainer:
• At least eight people were killed and several others injured in a building collapse in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.
• Emergency services, including NDRF and SDRF teams, were swiftly deployed for rescue operations, pulling out survivors from the debris.
• Preliminary investigations suggest possible structural weaknesses, unauthorized construction, or use of substandard materials as potential causes.
• The incident highlights persistent issues related to urban planning, building code enforcement, and construction safety standards in rapidly developing cities.
• A high-level inquiry has been ordered to ascertain the exact cause and fix accountability for the tragic loss of lives.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Disaster and disaster management
mainsPoints: Discuss the underlying reasons for frequent building collapses in Indian cities, focusing on issues related to urban governance, regulatory oversight, and construction practices.
Analyze the role of disaster management agencies and local authorities in preventing such incidents and ensuring effective rescue and relief operations.
Suggest comprehensive reforms in urban planning, building codes, and enforcement mechanisms to enhance public safety in urban infrastructure.
Examine the socio-economic impact of such disasters on affected families and the broader community.
GS1.imageDescription: Rescue workers searching through the debris of a collapsed building in Lucknow.
GS1.trivia: The National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) is a specialized force constituted for the purpose of specialist response to natural and man-made disasters.

GS2.headline: UPSC Dismisses Trainee IAS Officer for Fraudulent Methods in Exam
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) dismissed and canceled the candidature of trainee IAS officer Puja Khedkar.
• The dismissal stems from her use of fraudulent methods to clear the prestigious UPSC Civil Services Examination.
• This action underscores UPSC's strict stance against malpractices and its commitment to maintaining the integrity of the examination process.
• The incident highlights the importance of ethical conduct and honesty for individuals aspiring to join the civil services.
• Such cases often lead to systemic reviews within the examination and recruitment bodies to prevent future occurrences.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice and International relations
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies

Ethics and Human Interface: Essence, Determinants and Consequences of Ethics in-human actions; Dimensions of ethics; Ethics – in private and public relationships. Human Values – lessons from the lives and teachings of great leaders, reformers and administrators; role of family, society and educational institutions in inculcating values.

Probity in Governance: Concept of Public Service; Philosophical basis of governance and probity; Information sharing and transparency in government, Right to Information, Codes of Ethics, Codes of Conduct, Citizen’s Charters, Work culture, Quality of service delivery, Utilization of public funds, challenges of corruption.
prelimsPoints: UPSC dismissed trainee IAS officer Puja Khedkar.
Reason for dismissal: fraudulent methods in clearing the UPSC exam.
UPSC is a constitutional body responsible for civil services recruitment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of integrity and ethical conduct in the civil services and the role of bodies like UPSC in upholding these values.
Analyze the implications of fraudulent practices in competitive examinations on public trust and the quality of governance.
Suggest measures to further strengthen the examination system and prevent malpractices, ensuring fairness and transparency.
Examine the ethical dilemmas faced by aspirants and the importance of a strong moral compass for future administrators (GS4 relevance).
GS2.imageDescription: UPSC building in New Delhi, symbolizing integrity and governance.
GS2.trivia: The UPSC conducts one of the toughest examinations globally, with a success rate typically less than 0.2% annually.

GS2.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Enhanced Strategic Partnership at ASEAN Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India participated in the annual ASEAN Summit, reaffirming its commitment to strengthening the comprehensive strategic partnership with the bloc.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in trade, investment, digital transformation, connectivity, and maritime security.
• India's 'Act East Policy' was highlighted as a cornerstone of its engagement with Southeast Asian nations, promoting regional stability and prosperity.
• Leaders emphasized the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, with ASEAN centrality as a key principle.
• New initiatives were explored to boost people-to-people exchanges, cultural ties, and cooperation in sustainable development and climate action.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India is a dialogue partner of ASEAN and has a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
ASEAN consists of 10 Southeast Asian nations.
India's 'Act East Policy' aims to strengthen ties with ASEAN countries.
The concept of Indo-Pacific and ASEAN centrality are key themes in India-ASEAN relations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in the context of its strategic engagement with ASEAN.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India-ASEAN cooperation in areas such as trade, maritime security, and digital economy.
Examine how India and ASEAN can collectively contribute to a stable, prosperous, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the impact of global geopolitical shifts on India's relations with Southeast Asian countries.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders from India and ASEAN nations at a summit meeting.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by its five founding members.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Seeks Centre's Response on Electoral Bond Transparency Pleas
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India sought the Central government's response on several petitions challenging the legality and transparency of the Electoral Bond Scheme.
• The pleas argue that the scheme promotes opacity in political funding, violating citizens' right to information and free and fair elections.
• The Election Commission of India has also previously raised concerns about the lack of transparency under the scheme.
• This renewed judicial scrutiny comes amidst ongoing debates about electoral reforms and the need for greater accountability in political financing.
• The court's decision will have significant implications for the future of political funding in India and the democratic process.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court sought Centre's response on Electoral Bond Scheme.
Petitions challenge the scheme on grounds of transparency and right to information.
Election Commission of India has also expressed concerns about the scheme.
Electoral bonds are financial instruments for making donations to political parties.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the arguments for and against the Electoral Bond Scheme in the context of transparency, accountability, and democratic principles.
Discuss the role of the judiciary and the Election Commission in safeguarding the integrity of the electoral process and ensuring free and fair elections.
Examine various proposals for electoral reforms in India, particularly concerning political funding, and their potential impact.
Evaluate the balance between protecting donor privacy and ensuring public accountability in political donations.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The Electoral Bond Scheme was introduced in 2017 through the Finance Bill, 2017, and came into effect in 2018.

GS2.headline: Progress Made on India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor Projects
GS2.explainer:
• A review meeting confirmed significant progress on various projects under the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC).
• IMEC, announced at the G20 Summit, aims to establish a vast network of rail, road, and sea linkages connecting India with Europe via the Middle East.
• The corridor is envisioned to enhance trade, improve logistics efficiency, and foster economic integration among participating nations.
• Discussions focused on accelerating infrastructure development, streamlining customs procedures, and mobilizing investments.
• The project holds strategic geopolitical significance, offering an alternative trade route and strengthening India's connectivity with key global regions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: IMEC is the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
It was announced at the G20 Summit.
Aims to connect India, Middle East, and Europe via rail, road, and sea.
Key objective is to enhance trade and logistics efficiency.
mainsPoints: Analyze the geopolitical and geo-economic significance of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) for India and the wider region.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges in the implementation of the IMEC, including infrastructure development, funding, and regional cooperation.
Compare and contrast IMEC with other major connectivity initiatives like China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI), highlighting their strategic implications.
Evaluate how IMEC can contribute to India's economic growth, trade diversification, and strategic autonomy.
GS2.imageDescription: Map illustrating the proposed route of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
GS2.trivia: The IMEC project was initially conceptualized as part of the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment (PGII), a G7 initiative to counter China's BRI.

GS2.headline: G20 Sherpas Discuss Agenda for Upcoming Leaders' Summit
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Sherpas convened a crucial meeting to finalize the agenda and outcome documents for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions covered a wide range of global issues, including climate finance, sustainable development goals (SDGs), global debt, and digital public infrastructure.
• India, as a significant voice in the Global South, emphasized inclusive growth, equitable access to technology, and reforms in multilateral institutions.
• The Sherpas worked towards building consensus on key declarations and action plans that will guide global cooperation in the coming year.
• This preparatory meeting is vital for shaping the deliverables and overall success of the high-level G20 summit.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: G20 Sherpas meet to prepare for the Leaders' Summit.
Sherpas are personal representatives of the leaders of G20 countries.
Key agenda items include climate finance, SDGs, and global debt.
G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving role of the G20 as a premier forum for international economic cooperation and its significance for India's foreign policy.
Analyze the challenges in achieving consensus among G20 members on critical global issues such as climate change, trade, and debt sustainability.
Evaluate India's contributions and priorities within the G20 framework, particularly its advocacy for the Global South and reforms in global governance.
Examine the 'Sherpa track' process in global summits and its importance in diplomatic negotiations and outcome finalization.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo displayed at a conference venue.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Sherpa' in diplomacy refers to the personal representatives of the leaders who prepare the agenda and negotiate the outcome of international summits.

GS3.headline: Historic Tripura Peace Accord Signed, Ends Decades-Old Insurgency
GS3.explainer:
• A landmark peace accord was signed between the National Liberation Front of Tripura (NLFT) and the All Tripura Tiger Force (ATTF) on one side, and the State Government of Tripura and the Government of India on the other.
• The agreement marks the end of a 35-year-old insurgency in Tripura, bringing hopes for lasting peace and development in the region.
• The accord includes provisions for the rehabilitation and resettlement of former militants, surrender of arms, and integration into the mainstream.
• It emphasizes socio-economic development, protection of indigenous rights, and addressing the root causes of the insurgency.
• This follows a series of peace initiatives by the government in the North-Eastern states to resolve long-standing ethnic and political conflicts.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: The 2024 Tripura Peace Accord was signed on September 4, 2024.
Signatories include NLFT, ATTF, Government of Tripura, and Government of India.
The accord aims to end a 35-year insurgency in Tripura.
It focuses on rehabilitation, development, and protection of indigenous rights.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of peace accords in resolving insurgencies in North-East India, citing the Tripura accord as an example.
Analyze the multi-faceted approach required for sustainable peace, including rehabilitation, development, and addressing socio-economic grievances.
Evaluate the role of both central and state governments in fostering dialogue and achieving resolution in conflict-affected regions.
Examine the challenges that remain post-accord, such as ensuring full integration of former militants and sustained development.
GS3.imageDescription: Signing ceremony of the Tripura Peace Accord with government officials and representatives of militant groups.
GS3.trivia: Tripura shares a long international border with Bangladesh, which has historically complicated its internal security situation due to cross-border movement of insurgents.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues related to Security and Governance

GS3.headline: Fresh Clashes in Manipur's Jiribam District Claim Five Lives
GS3.explainer:
• At least five people were killed in renewed clashes between the Kuki and Meitei communities in Jiribam District, Manipur.
• The violence is part of the ongoing 2023–2025 Manipur violence, which has seen intermittent but severe ethnic strife.
• Jiribam, bordering Assam, has recently emerged as a new flashpoint in the conflict, previously being relatively peaceful.
• Security forces have been deployed, but the underlying tensions and cycle of violence continue to pose significant challenges to peace and order.
• The incident highlights the urgent need for a lasting political solution and effective security measures to de-escalate the conflict.
gsSubTopics: Internal Security
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: New clashes occurred in Jiribam District, Manipur.
Conflict involves Kuki and Meitei communities.
Jiribam is a district bordering Assam in Manipur.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complex socio-political and historical factors contributing to the ongoing ethnic violence in Manipur.
Discuss the challenges faced by security forces and the administration in controlling the violence and restoring peace.
Evaluate the role of state and central governments in addressing the root causes of the conflict and ensuring rehabilitation of affected communities.
Suggest long-term strategies for peacebuilding and reconciliation in conflict-ridden regions like Manipur.
GS3.imageDescription: Security personnel patrolling a village affected by ethnic clashes in Manipur.
GS3.trivia: Manipur is known as the 'Land of Jewels' and is one of the seven sister states in Northeast India, rich in biodiversity and cultural heritage.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focuses on Inflation Moderation
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision aligns with the RBI's primary objective of bringing inflation within the target range while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC emphasized the need for continued vigilance on inflation, especially given global uncertainties and domestic food price pressures.
• Projections for GDP growth and inflation were reiterated, with a cautious optimism about the trajectory of the Indian economy.
• This 'wait and watch' approach signals a data-dependent stance, indicating future policy actions will be guided by evolving economic indicators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged.
Primary objective of MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation targeting framework in India aims for 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in managing inflation, particularly due to supply-side factors like food price shocks.
Analyze the implications of a 'status quo' monetary policy on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Examine the role of fiscal policy in complementing monetary policy for achieving macroeconomic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Key Parachute Deployment Test for Gaganyaan Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test of the parachute deployment system for its Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission.
• This test is a critical milestone, validating the system designed to safely bring back the crew module and astronauts to Earth.
• The multi-parachute system ensures controlled descent and soft landing, a vital aspect of crew safety for India's maiden human space mission.
• Gaganyaan aims to send a three-member crew to a Low Earth Orbit (LEO) for three days and bring them back safely to Earth.
• This achievement reinforces India's growing capabilities in advanced space technology and its commitment to indigenous human spaceflight.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested the parachute deployment system for Gaganyaan.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission.
The mission aims to send a three-member crew to Low Earth Orbit.
The parachute system is crucial for the safe return and soft landing of the crew module.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Gaganyaan mission for India's space program and its broader implications for national development and global standing.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in human spaceflight, particularly focusing on re-entry and crew recovery systems.
Evaluate the role of indigenous development in achieving self-reliance in critical space technologies and its impact on India's scientific capabilities.
Examine the potential spin-off benefits of human spaceflight missions for various sectors, including healthcare, materials science, and education.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of the Gaganyaan crew module descending with parachutes deployed.
GS3.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission's 'Vyommitra' is a female humanoid robot developed by ISRO, designed to fly aboard the uncrewed test flights to simulate human functions and monitor parameters.

GS3.headline: Government Approves New Incentives to Boost Green Hydrogen Production
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new set of incentives and policy frameworks under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The move aims to accelerate domestic production of green hydrogen and its derivatives, positioning India as a global hub.
• Incentives include financial support for manufacturing electrolyzers and producing green hydrogen, alongside demand aggregation mechanisms.
• This initiative is crucial for India's energy security, reducing reliance on fossil fuel imports, and achieving net-zero emission targets by 2070.
• The mission is expected to create significant employment opportunities and foster technological innovation in the renewable energy sector.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Government approved new incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Aim is to boost green hydrogen production and electrolyzer manufacturing.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India targets net-zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and its role in achieving climate change mitigation targets.
Analyze the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production in India, including infrastructure, cost, and technological advancements.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government incentives and policy frameworks in attracting investment and fostering innovation in the green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen adoption on various industrial sectors and its contribution to job creation.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but 'green' hydrogen specifically refers to hydrogen produced with zero carbon emissions.

GS3.headline: India Crosses Milestone in Digital Public Infrastructure Adoption
GS3.explainer:
• India announced a significant milestone in the adoption and scaling of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) platforms, including UPI, Aadhaar, and DigiLocker.
• The increased penetration reflects the success of government initiatives to promote financial inclusion, enhance public service delivery, and foster digital literacy.
• India's DPI model is gaining global recognition, with several countries expressing interest in replicating its successful frameworks.
• The expansion of platforms like ONDC (Open Network for Digital Commerce) is further democratizing digital markets and empowering small businesses.
• This widespread adoption is transforming various sectors, from banking and healthcare to education and governance, making services more accessible and efficient.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Policies & Interventions for Development in various sectors and issues arising out of their Design and Implementation
prelimsPoints: India achieved a milestone in Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) adoption.
Key DPIs include UPI, Aadhaar, DigiLocker, and ONDC.
DPI aims to promote financial inclusion, public service delivery, and digital literacy.
India's DPI model is being recognized globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss how India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model has revolutionized financial inclusion and public service delivery, citing specific examples.
Analyze the opportunities and challenges in expanding India's DPI footprint globally, particularly in developing countries.
Evaluate the role of DPI in fostering innovation, economic growth, and social empowerment across various sectors.
Examine the ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with large-scale DPI implementation and suggest measures for robust governance.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the interconnectedness of various Digital Public Infrastructure platforms like UPI and Aadhaar.
GS3.trivia: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) has set global benchmarks for real-time digital payments, processing billions of transactions monthly.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Levels of Air Pollution in Indian Cities
GS3.explainer:
• A recent report revealed alarming levels of air pollution, particularly PM2.5 concentrations, in several major Indian cities.
• The report highlighted the severe health impacts on urban populations, including respiratory diseases, cardiovascular issues, and reduced life expectancy.
• Key contributors to urban air pollution include vehicular emissions, industrial activities, construction dust, and biomass burning.
• The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) and other policy interventions are in place, but their implementation and effectiveness require significant strengthening.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for comprehensive, multi-sectoral strategies to improve air quality and protect public health.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Report highlights high PM2.5 levels in Indian cities.
PM2.5 refers to fine particulate matter 2.5 micrometers or less in diameter.
National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) is a government initiative to reduce air pollution.
Sources of air pollution include vehicles, industry, construction, and biomass burning.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes and consequences of escalating urban air pollution in India, particularly focusing on PM2.5 levels.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of existing government policies and programs, such as NCAP, in addressing air quality challenges.
Suggest comprehensive and integrated strategies, including technological, behavioral, and policy interventions, to mitigate air pollution in Indian cities.
Discuss the socio-economic implications of air pollution, including its impact on public health, productivity, and environmental justice.
GS3.imageDescription: Smoggy skyline of an Indian city, depicting severe air pollution.
GS3.trivia: Exposure to PM2.5 for even a few hours can cause respiratory and cardiac problems, while long-term exposure can lead to chronic diseases and premature death.

GS3.headline: DAC Clears Major Defence Acquisitions to Boost Indigenous Production
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, approved several significant defence acquisition proposals.
• These approvals are aimed at bolstering the Indian armed forces' capabilities and promoting indigenous defence manufacturing under the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative.
• The procurements include advanced weapon systems, surveillance equipment, and platforms, with a strong emphasis on 'Make in India' components.
• The move is expected to reduce India's reliance on foreign defence imports and foster a robust domestic defence industrial base.
• Such decisions are crucial for India's strategic autonomy and enhancing its defence preparedness in the evolving geopolitical landscape.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) approves defence procurements.
DAC is chaired by the Defence Minister.
Focus on 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and 'Make in India' in defence.
Aims to reduce import dependence and boost indigenous production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and impact of India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative in the defence sector on national security and economic development.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in enhancing indigenous defence manufacturing, including technology transfer, R&D, and private sector participation.
Evaluate how recent defence acquisition policies contribute to India's strategic autonomy and its standing as a regional power.
Examine the role of the Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) in streamlining the procurement process and ensuring transparency and efficiency.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Defence Minister reviewing military equipment at a defence exhibition.
GS3.trivia: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body in the Defence Ministry for deciding on new policies and capital acquisitions for the three services (Army, Navy, Air Force) and the Indian Coast Guard.

GS3.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Stricter Measures for Western Ghats Conservation
GS3.explainer:
• An expert committee submitted recommendations for stricter environmental protection measures in the ecologically sensitive Western Ghats region.
• The panel emphasized the need to balance conservation efforts with the developmental aspirations of local communities.
• Recommendations include stricter regulations on mining, quarrying, and construction activities in designated ecologically sensitive areas (ESAs).
• The report revisits aspects of previous committees (Gadgil and Kasturirangan) on the Western Ghats, aiming for a more implementable framework.
